calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-30-2019 Off Calendar

Amended Notice Of Hearing For Further Motion To Compel And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-30-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Thomas Kelly III of the L/O of Thomas Kelly, T:707.545.8700, plaintiff and moving party appearing on his own behalf, by Court Call;
No other appearance.

Plaintiff stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING: The tentative ruling was discussed. There being no objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF THOMAS KELLY's Amended Motion To Compel And For Sanctions, as follows:

Plaintiff Thomas P. Kelly's ("Kelly's") Further Motion to Compel Further Response to Subpoena by Yelp Inc. and for Sanctions is DENIED as MOOT. Non-Party Yelp Inc. ("Yelp") declines to seek monetary sanctions and thus any such sanctions are DENIED as UNSOUGHT. All other sanctions are DENIED. As the prevailing party, Yelp shall prepare a form of the order recapitulating this ruling, and complies with Cal. R. Ct. 3.1312.

Earlier this year, Kelly moved this court to compel his subpoena against Yelp to reveal certain information about a third party, known at the time only as "Michael L." Kelly's subpoena sought the following information about Michael L: "name, address, contact information, documents referring to that person, [and] communications between [Michael L.] and Yelp." Order Granting Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Response to Subpoena by Yelp Inc. and for Sanctions, at 1:6-9, May 30, 2019 [hereinafter Judge Schulman's Order]. In May 2019, some four months ago, this Court granted Kelley's motion compelling Yelp to respond to Kelley's subpoena, but limiting that response to revealing Michael L's "identifying information." Id. at 1:1-3. Yelp appealed the decision. The California Court of Appeal affirmed Judge Schulman's Order.

Kelly argues in the Instant Motion that Yelp as failed to comply with the Judge Schulman's Order. It is true that as of the time of the filing of the Motion, Yelp had not complied with Judge Schulman's Order. But this is because, as of the fling of the Instant Motion, on July 23, 2019, the action was stayed pending a decision by the California Court of Appeal. The Court of Appeal decided the issue on September 5, 2019, denying Yelp's request for relief and affirming the trial court's order. The next day, September 6, 2019, Yelp did, in fact, comply with Judge Schulman's Order, providing Michael L's identifying information. Kelly fails to submit a Reply controverting these facts.

Judge Pro Tem: Douglas Robbins; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-28-2019 Continued To Sep-18-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion And Notice Of Motion To Compel Response To Subpoena By Yelp Inc. And For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-30-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Kelly III, Esq. 707-542-3373 for Plaintiff through Court Call.
William Frimel, Esq. 650-322-3048 for Other/Non-party Yelp.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court announces the reasons for the tentative ruling. Counsel for Non-party argues prima facie showing re: evidence. Plaintiff responds. The Court grants Yelp's request to limit the request to the reviewer's contact information.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF THOMAS KELLY's Motion To Compel Response To Subpoena By Yelp Inc. And For Sanctions.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling and modifies the order to limit the request to the reviewer'sidentifying information. The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Plaintiff Thomas P. Kelly III's motion to compel Yelp Inc. to respond to Plaintiff's records subpoena is granted limited to the reviewer's identifying informtaion. Both parties' requests for sanctions are denied. (see word minutes for full details) (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Motion And Notice Of Motion To Compel Response To Subpoena By Yelp Inc. And For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-13-2019 Continued To May-30-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Thomas P. Kelly, Jr., esq. (707-542-3373) for Plaintiff via Court Call.
William Frimel, esq. (650-322-3048) of Scubert French Frimel + Warner for Non-Party, Yelp.

Counsel for Yelp does not stipulate to the matter being heard by the Judge Pro Tem.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF THOMAS KELLY's Motion To Compel Response To Subpoena By Yelp Inc. And For Sanctions

The matter is continued to May 30, 2019 at 9:30 am in Department 302, Law and Motion Department.
Judge Pro Tem to prepare report and recommendation.
